warningMessage: Increased risk of bleeding when Aspirin is combined with Warfarin.
Ibuprofen may reduce the cardioprotective effect of low-dose Aspirin.
ACE inhibitors with potassium supplements can cause hyperkalemia.
Alcohol increases the risk of lactic acidosis with Metformin.
Risk of rhabdomyolysis — limit Simvastatin dose to 20mg daily.
Antacids reduce absorption of Ciprofloxacin. Take 2 hours apart.
Combining beta-blockers with calcium channel blockers may cause severe bradycardia.
Omeprazole may reduce the effectiveness of Clopidogrel.
Risk of serotonin syndrome when SSRIs are combined with Tramadol.
Amiodarone increases Digoxin levels — monitor closely and reduce dose.
NSAIDs increase lithium levels, risk of toxicity.
High-dose Paracetamol may slightly enhance Warfarin effect.
Amlodipine increases Simvastatin levels. Limit Simvastatin to 20mg.
Severe nausea, vomiting, and flushing — avoid alcohol completely.
ARBs with potassium supplements increase hyperkalemia risk.
